reviews.review_msg: Sam is an average professor, but not the worst. For the final exam, we've been given no information about what to expect or how it will be structured. This has made it very difficult to study, since I'm unsure what to focus on. At this point, it feels like the exam will be a complete surprise, and I don't know what to expect.

Sam was not ideal, he was not the best but I have definitely had worse, refusing to give the class any information about the final exam is an interesting choice, and lectures were not well explained and he often would not know the answer to peoples questions. I found the content to be interesting enough but more real world context would have helped

I wish we had Sam for the entire semester. I was unfortunately only able to go to half his lectures but when I did go he explained things very well. There are a lot of complex processes in his part of the course but he taught them well. His questions were hard but super fair, he didn't throw any curveballs like you see in other BPK courses.
Good professor. Teaches very well and his testing makes sense. I wish he taught the whole course instead of just the second half.

His lectures are ok. The problem was the questions on the exam. It felt very lackluster and felt that very little effort was put in for the exam. The difference in quality between Nadine's exam questions and Sam's is very clear. If he is teaching for the semester, it would be a good idea to skipped that term.
Good prof. Exams questions match the lectures but are more like 305 than 306. Go to lectures and to office hours.

Dr. Doesburg is very informative and I enjoyed his lectures. The second half of this course is very complicated so I don't think that's his fault. His pacing is good, it leaves me enough time to jot down important notes. Whenever I ask him a question, he answers directly and clearly which I appreciate!

Reads off the slides. Im basically falling asleep in class. He doesnt really explain topics that well. He goes off topic quite often. Also talks very quick which makes it hard to get notes down and we get off class 8-10 mins earlier than normal. Wished he would use that time to break down more complex topics.

His exams had a large amount of grammatical errors that made it extremely difficult to answer questions, MCQ were multiple paragraphs in a 50 minute exam? I understand that English may not be your first language but then someone needs to check grammar. He also gave the TA a version of the test that was grammatically correct and different from ours

Clearly all reviews on March 26 were made by him. For midterm 1 & 2, his MC questions were very disorganized. On MT2, his 2nd written question had almost everyone stumped and very confused. Also, since MC from MT2 was mostly by him, the TA reported that its where most people lost marks. He does not know how to test and teach.
Professor Balbinot is enthusiastic about the science and does a great job communications complex topics. This professor is not for beginners, you have to study hard!
Professor Gustavo is one of the best professor I ever had. He really work hard to deliver a comprehensive, high level talk. Highly recommended.
Recommended if you are the kind of student interested in science and work hard. If you do not apply yourself and study hard, you will fail this course.

I do not suggest taking BPK 306 with this guy. There are default slides that the BPK profs use for this course along with 205 and 305 but he adds on his own information onto the slides or adds new slides that expand on the material. This rarely makes it easier because it just forces you to have to study more concepts in an already heavy course.

He certainly knows his material, however, this is his first time teaching one of the most important courses for BPK. I feel that this was too quick of a jump because in our most recent lecture he found himself going to slow for the amount of material he must deliver within 50 minutes. It was kinda rushed and harder to understand with his accent...

Dr Kreiger seems like a nice guy but he's not great at explaining topics. He goes off on random topics in detail that are not even part of the exam and then doesn't address the actual testable information. He speaks with his back turned to you and often confuses himself and the students.
His lectures were extremely boring and dry. Absolutely no interactivity. Imagine you are listening to the world's most boring podcast and the exam is on the smallest detail.

His exams are very difficult to predict and require you to extend your knowledge. Even after studying daily and keeping up with the material, I felt the final was rough. But overall, he seems like a really nice guy and he is there to help you.

Course material is not hard, but it doesn't matter because that's not what is on the exams. Exams are riddled with typos and ask irrelevant questions (e.g. 7/20 points on the 306 midterm went to biomechanical questions). Will tell you some material won't be on exams and yet he'll test it anyway. Used 3 textbooks (costly!), and all were examinable.

Charles is an awesome guy but his tests are confusing. Good luck trying to predict whats going to be on the midterm. However, if you go get your exam remarked you might score some extra points. He is very friendly but will take 10 minutes to explain a question that you should be able to answer in one sentence on an exam, make sense, no!

He was horrible at preparing you for exams. I would recommend going to see him after every lecture to clarify what the main points are and try to get sample questions he will actually use. I worked extremely hard in this class and still ended up failing.

Hate him or love him he will remain as long as his wife is dean! He confuses himself in his own lectures, if he put a few words on his slides it might even help him! Wastes a lot of time in class, dumps a lot of material on you but fair grader and friendly to talk to. This course was more work than it was worth though.
